title: The Foxglove King
author: Hannah Whitten
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Foxglove King" book by Hannah Whitten?
Options:
A. Those in authority were troubled by favored successors ready to assume their roles. The Court of the Citadel resembled a poison dealer group in that way. She had witnessed more than one ambitious person murdered by their own leader. A wide yawn made her jaw pop. She had barely noticed the walk from the Citadel's back entrance to the North Sanctuary, too exhausted to focus. By her estimate it was over a mile, with cobblestone paving and rose bushes - unlike the rubble-covered paths in Dellaire itself that led to the South Sanctuary for regular people.  
B. Those with authority were unsettled by chosen successors ready to replace them. In that way, the Court of the Citadel resembled a poison-dealing crew. She had witnessed several ambitious up-and-comers murdered by their own captain. A wide yawn made her jaw pop. She had barely noticed the walk from the Citadel's rear entrance to the North Sanctuary, too exhausted to focus. It was around a mile and a half by her estimate, the path cobblestone and smooth, lined with rose bushes - unlike the rubble-filled walkways in Dellaire itself to the South Sanctuary for the masses.
C. Powerful people felt threatened by chosen heirs who were waiting to take over. The Court of the Citadel was similar to a poison selling gang in that respect. She had seen multiple upcoming leaders killed by their own boss. A big yawn made her jaw crack. She had hardly paid attention on the walk from the back of the Citadel to the North Sanctuary, too tired to concentrate. It was about a mile and a half by her guess, with cobblestone pavement and rosebushes - very different from the rubble-filled paths in Dellaire that went to the South Sanctuary meant for common folk.
D. Men in powerful positions were unsettled by popular heirs waiting to take their places. In that regard, the Court of the Citadel wasnt that much different from a poison runner crew. Shed seen more than one upstart assassinated by their own captain. A yawn stretched her mouth so wide Lores jaw popped. Shed barely taken in the walk from the back entrance of the Citadel to the North Sanctuary, too tired to pay much attention. It was a good mile and a half, by her counting, the path cobble-paved and smooth, lined with rosebushesa stark contrast with the rubble-strewn walkways in Dellaire proper leading to the South Sanctuary, the one meant for commoners.
Answer:
